data.title: Rocket Lab Completes First 2026 Launch, Marks 80th Electron Mission
data.description:
Rocket Lab Corp (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares are up on Friday as the company recently completed its first launch of 2026, marking its 80th Electron mission overall. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Rocket Lab shares are under pressure. Why is RKLB stock trading lower?

Rocket Lab Solidifies Its Market Position

The mission, titled “The Cosmos Will See You Now”, successfully deployed two satellites into a 1,050 km circular orbit for Open Cosmos, a pan-European space tech firm. This launch reinforces Rocket Lab’s reputation as a reliable small satellite launch vehicle, with a full schedule of Electron launches planned for 2026.

Additionally, the company faced some challenges as its Neutron rocket’s Stage 1 tank ruptured during testing, which caused a temporary drop in stock price. However, Rocket Lab confirmed that no significant damage occurred, and production of the next Stage 1 tank is already underway.

Rocket Lab’s Unstoppable Momentum: What Lies Ahead?

Rocket Lab is currently trading 5.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 46.7% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating strong short-term and longer-term strength. Over the past 12 months, shares have surged 176.78% and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 62.26, indicating neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, suggesting bearish pressure. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $99.50
  • Key Support: $84.00

Earnings Report: A Turning Point Ahead?

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Feb. 26.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 9 cents (Up from Loss of 10 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $178.07 million (Up from $132.39 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $64.00. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Raises Target to $69.00) (Jan. 20)
  • B of A Securities: Buy (Raises Target to $120.00) (Jan. 20)
  • Morgan Stanley: Upgraded to Overweight (Raises Target to $105.00) (Jan. 16)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Rocket Lab, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 96.6) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Rocket Lab’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the market. Investors should keep an eye on upcoming earnings as the company continues to navigate its growth trajectory.

Top ETF Exposure

  • SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (NYSE:XAR): 5.66% Weight
  • First Trust Indxx Aerospace & Defense ETF (NYSE:MISL): 4.81% Weight
  • ARK Space & Defense Innovation ETF (NASDAQ:ARKX): 7.62% Weight

Significance: Because RKLB carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

RKLB Shares Trade Flat Friday

RKLB Price Action: Rocket Lab shares were up 2.73% at $90.39 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Datavault Shares Reverses After Closing API Acquisition
data.description:
Datavault AI Inc. (NASDAQ:DVLT) shares were trading higher Friday but have since reversed and began trading lower after the company announced it completed its previously disclosed acquisition of API Media Innovation Inc.

Datavault Closes Acquisition of API Media

Datavault said the acquisition expands its proprietary data-monetization ecosystem and extends its patented ADIO technology into the live outdoor event market. API provides media infrastructure and event technology solutions and brings expertise in multi-channel engagement, data overlay integration, and automated media operations.

CEO Nathaniel Bradley said the transaction represents a key step in Datavault’s strategy to scale its platform and enhance the value delivered to enterprise clients through verified, tokenized data assets. The company said API Media's brand and operational capabilities are expected to strengthen its core platform and support growth across enterprise data, advertising, and AI-as-a-Service verticals.

API President Frank Tomaino said the combination will allow clients to access advanced AI, analytics, and secure data monetization capabilities, while API CEO David Reese said the integration creates opportunities to accelerate Datavault's progress and build new strategic relationships.

Technical Analysis Of Datavault

The stock is currently trading 12.6% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 26.9% below its 50-day SMA, indicating short-term strength while struggling with longer-term trends. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 25.23% and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing challenges for the stock.

The RSI is at 46.52, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is not currently overbought or oversold. Meanwhile, the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating potential for upward movement if buying interest continues.

DVLT Price Action: At the time of writing, Datavault shares are trading 5.57% lower at 84 cents, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: How To Trade SPY, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, TSLA Using Technical Analysis
data.description:
Good Morning Traders!

Today's session rounds out the shortened holiday week with a relatively light economic calendar. At 9:45AM ET, markets will focus on the January Preliminary S&P Global Manufacturing PMI, Services PMI, and Composite PMI, which offer an early read on business activity and demand conditions. These releases can influence sentiment, especially with traders looking for confirmation that growth remains stable heading into the next month. 

At 10:00AM ET, attention shifts to the Revised University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment and Inflation Expectations. These figures are closely watched for insight into household confidence and inflation outlook, which can impact rate expectations. With liquidity still thinner than normal, price action may be reactive and uneven.

Now, we will discuss SPY, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, and TSLA.

SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY)

SPY is currently trading around 688.25, where buyers are trying to stabilize price after last week's range expansion. Holding above this area keeps the door open for a push into 690.00, where momentum could build toward 691.75 if buyers stay engaged. Strength above that zone would suggest continuation potential as flows return after the shortened holiday week, with upside extension possible into the low 693s if risk appetite improves. 

If SPY loses 688.25, sellers may begin pressing price back into 686.75, where buyers will need to respond quickly. Failure to hold that level opens downside toward 685.00, and sustained pressure could drag price into 683.25. A break below that region would signal fading demand and increase the odds of a deeper rotation lower as liquidity remains thin.

Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (QQQ)

QQQ is currently trading around 620.00, with bulls attempting to reclaim control following recent consolidation. Holding this level allows for a move into 622.25, where acceptance could fuel continuation toward 624.50. If tech leadership firms up, price may extend into 626.75, especially if buyers step in aggressively on shallow pullbacks. 

If QQQ slips below 620.00, downside pressure may develop quickly into 618.50. Losing that level puts 616.75 in play, and continued weakness could target 615.00. Failure to find support there would suggest sellers regaining control as growth names lose momentum.

Apple Inc. (AAPL)

Apple is currently trading around 248.25, where buyers are attempting to defend a key short term pivot. Holding this area could allow price to work higher into 249.75, with follow through potentially carrying into 251.50 if buyers build confidence. A stronger tape may open a path toward 253.00 as dip buyers step back in. 

If AAPL fails to hold 248.25, sellers may press price into 246.75. Continued weakness could slide into 245.00, and if that level breaks, downside risk increases toward 243.50. A lack of buyer response would signal caution returning to large cap leadership.

Microsoft Corp. MSFT)

Microsoft is currently trading around 451.25, as buyers look to establish support after recent volatility. Holding this level keeps upside targets at 453.25 and 455.00 in play. If momentum builds, price may extend toward 457.50, signaling renewed strength within mega cap tech. 

If MSFT breaks below 451.25, sellers could drive price into 449.75. Losing that level exposes 447.50, and sustained selling pressure may test 445.25. Weak follow through here would reflect broader hesitation across software names.

NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA)

NVIDIA is currently trading around 187.50, with buyers attempting to stabilize after sharp recent moves. Holding above this zone allows for a bounce into 189.25, where acceptance could lead to continuation toward 191.50. A stronger push may stretch price into 193.75 if momentum returns to semiconductors. 

If NVDA loses 187.50, sellers may push price into 185.75. Failure to hold there opens downside toward 183.50, with further weakness potentially reaching 181.25. A break of those levels would suggest risk appetite fading within high beta tech.

Alphabet Inc Class A (GOOGL)

Alphabet is currently trading around 330.50, where buyers are attempting to defend a key consolidation area. Holding support here could allow price to lift into 332.75, with continuation possible toward 335.00 if buyers stay active. Strength through that zone would indicate improving sentiment within communication services. 

If GOOGL slips below 330.50, sellers may pressure price into 328.75. Continued weakness could test 326.50, and a failure there opens downside risk toward 324.25. Sellers gaining traction here would point to broader market hesitation.

Meta Platforms Inc (META)

Meta is currently trading around 644.75, with bulls trying to reclaim momentum after recent consolidation. Holding this area keeps upside targets at 647.50 and 650.25 in play. Strong follow through could allow price to extend toward 653.75 as buyers reassert control. 

If META loses 644.75, sellers may push price into 642.00. Continued downside pressure could reach 639.25, and further weakness may target 636.50. A lack of support at these levels would suggest momentum shifting back to sellers.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA)

Tesla is currently trading around 449.25, where buyers are attempting to establish a higher base. Holding above this level could allow price to move into 452.00, with continuation possible toward 455.50 if momentum builds. A strong tape may open the door toward 459.00 as volatility expands. 

If TSLA breaks below 449.25, sellers may quickly test 446.50. Continued weakness could drag price into 443.75, and failure there increases risk toward 440.25. Heavy selling at those levels would reflect renewed pressure in high beta names.

Final Word: Traders should stay disciplined, respect levels, and be prepared for quick rotations. Good luck, happy Friday, trade safely, and have a great weekend!

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Atlantic Buys Europe's Circle8 To Create $1.2 Billion Global Hiring Giant
data.description:
On Friday, Atlantic International Corp. (NASDAQ:ATLN) is pushing deeper into technology staffing with its acquisition of Europe-based Circle8 Group, expanding into a cross-Atlantic workforce platform spanning industrial and IT talent across North America and Europe. Financial terms were not disclosed.

Shares of Atlantic traded lower on Friday following the announcement. The deal comes as Atlantic seeks to diversify its staffing mix and scale internationally.

The all-stock transaction pairs Atlantic's North American light-industrial staffing operations with Circle8's European IT and technology recruiting business, creating a combined platform Atlantic values at approximately $1.2 billion in unaudited annual revenue.

The acquisition adds exposure to higher-growth, typically higher-margin technology placements alongside Atlantic's traditional industrial staffing.

Circle8 generated roughly $780 million in unaudited revenue in 2025, and Atlantic said the business is positioned to reach $1 billion in organic revenue in 2026. Operating across Europe through multiple brands, Circle8 manages more than 12,000 technology professionals, giving Atlantic a meaningful European footprint and expanded access to multinational clients.

Circle8 founder and CEO Guus Franke will continue to lead the business and will join Atlantic's board as executive chairman, a structure Atlantic says preserves operating momentum while aligning leadership incentives.

CEO Jeffrey Jagid called the deal an accelerant to Atlantic's global scaling strategy, while Franke said the combination broadens coverage across continents and talent categories, creating a more resilient workforce platform for global enterprises.

ATLN Price Action: Atlantic International shares were down 15.20% at $3.18 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
